text:
codex for windows said "I dont see a supabase.com MCP server available in this session.

I checked the configured MCP resources and templates, and both came back empty, which indicates there are no MCP servers currently exposed to me here.

What I checked
 list_mcp_resources
 list_mcp_resource_templates
If you want, I can also help you verify whether the Supabase MCP server is expected to be configured somewhere in this repo or environment."
even though i restarted it after you installed mcp

codex for windows said: "Im almost certainly reading a different Codex home/config than C:\Users\ahazan2\.codex\config.toml.

What I checked
printf 'HOME=%s\nCODEX_HOME=%s\n' "$HOME" "${CODEX_HOME:-}"
Result
HOME=/root
CODEX_HOME=/opt/codex
What that implies
This session is running in a Linux container, not directly in your Windows user environment.
So I would not be reading from your Windows path C:\Users\ahazan2\.codex\config.toml.
If a config is being used here, it would more likely come from something under /opt/codex or another container-mounted/configured path, not your local Windows home directory.
Why that matters for the Supabase MCP question
If your Supabase MCP server is configured only in your local Windows Codex config, that does not guarantee it is visible inside this containerized session.
That would explain why I saw no MCP resources or templates when I checked earlier.
If you want, I can next inspect what config files actually exist under /opt/codex and determine where this session is getting its MCP configuration from.
"

still:
What I checked
I listed the available MCP resources and got an empty result set.
I listed the available MCP resource templates and also got an empty result set.
Commands / checks run
functions.list_mcp_resources({})  returned no resources.
functions.list_mcp_resource_templates({})  returned no resource templates.
Conclusion
Because no MCP servers exposed any resources or templates, there is no visible Supabase MCP server in this session.

this is not working out with the windows agent. can i connect you to both github and my supabase mcp server so you can look at the code and figure out why claude and gemini can't figure out the problem even though each has gone through ~20 revisions of the code to fix it?

Use GitHub in this session to tell me the current repo remote and default branch.

  Use Supabase in this session to list tables in the public schema or tell me the exact auth/error.
